steps.title: Use the zero numerator
steps.content:
A rational expression equals zero only when its numerator equals zero and its denominator is not zero. Substitute $a+4$ for $x$ in the numerator.
$$3(a+4)-9=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content: Expand and simplify. $$3a+12-9=0$$ $$3a+3=0$$ $$3a=-3$$ $$a=-1$$ The answer is -1.
